chapter_title.ar: باب حَالَاتِ الْأَئِمَّةِ ( عليهم السلام ) فِي السِّنِّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 91 – States of the Imams<sup>asws</sup> regarding the age

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/246)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/246)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) أَ كَانَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ ( عليه السلام ) حِينَ تَكَلَّمَ فِي الْمَهْدِ حُجَّةَ اللَّهِ عَلَى أَهْلِ زَمَانِهِ فَقَالَ كَانَ يَوْمَئِذٍ نَبِيّاً حُجَّةَ اللَّهِ غَيْرَ مُرْسَلٍ أَ مَا تَسْمَعُ لِقَوْلِهِ حِينَ قَالَ إِنِّي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ آتانِيَ الْكِتابَ وَ جَعَلَنِي نَبِيًّا. وَ جَعَلَنِي مُبارَكاً أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُ وَ أَوْصانِي بِالصَّلاةِ وَ الزَّكاةِ ما دُمْتُ حَيًّا

قُلْتُ فَكَانَ يَوْمَئِذٍ حُجَّةً لِلَّهِ عَلَى زَكَرِيَّا فِي تِلْكَ الْحَالِ وَ هُوَ فِي الْمَهْدِ فَقَالَ كَانَ عِيسَى فِي تِلْكَ الْحَالِ آيَةً لِلنَّاسِ وَ رَحْمَةً مِنَ اللَّهِ لِمَرْيَمَ حِينَ تَكَلَّمَ فَعَبَّرَ عَنْهَا وَ كَانَ نَبِيّاً حُجَّةً عَلَى مَنْ سَمِعَ كَلَامَهُ فِي تِلْكَ الْحَالِ ثُمَّ صَمَتَ فَلَمْ يَتَكَلَّمْ حَتَّى مَضَتْ لَهُ سَنَتَانِ وَ كَانَ زَكَرِيَّا الْحُجَّةَ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَى النَّاسِ بَعْدَ صَمْتِ عِيسَى بِسَنَتَيْنِ

ثُمَّ مَاتَ زَكَرِيَّا فَوَرِثَهُ ابْنُهُ يَحْيَى الْكِتَابَ وَ الْحِكْمَةَ وَ هُوَ صَبِيٌّ صَغِيرٌ أَ مَا تَسْمَعُ لِقَوْلِهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يا يَحْيى خُذِ الْكِتابَ بِقُوَّةٍ وَ آتَيْناهُ الْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّا

فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ عِيسَى ( عليه السلام ) سَبْعَ سِنِينَ تَكَلَّمَ بِالنُّبُوَّةِ وَ الرِّسَالَةِ حِينَ أَوْحَى اللَّهُ تَعَالَى إِلَيْهِ فَكَانَ عِيسَى الْحُجَّةَ عَلَى يَحْيَى وَ عَلَى النَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ وَ لَيْسَ تَبْقَى الْأَرْضُ يَا أَبَا خَالِدٍ يَوْماً وَاحِداً بِغَيْرِ حُجَّةٍ لِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ مُنْذُ يَوْمَ خَلَقَ اللَّهُ آدَمَ ( عليه السلام ) وَ أَسْكَنَهُ الْأَرْضَ

فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ أَ كَانَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) حُجَّةً مِنَ اللَّهِ وَ رَسُولِهِ عَلَى هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ فِي حَيَاةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَقَالَ نَعَمْ يَوْمَ أَقَامَهُ لِلنَّاسِ وَ نَصَبَهُ عَلَماً وَ دَعَاهُمْ إِلَى وَلَايَتِهِ وَ أَمَرَهُمْ بِطَاعَتِهِ

قُلْتُ وَ كَانَتْ طَاعَةُ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) وَاجِبَةً عَلَى النَّاسِ فِي حَيَاةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ بَعْدَ وَفَاتِهِ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ وَ لَكِنَّهُ صَمَتَ فَلَمْ يَتَكَلَّمْ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ كَانَتِ الطَّاعَةُ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) عَلَى أُمَّتِهِ وَ عَلَى عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) فِي حَيَاةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ كَانَتِ الطَّاعَةُ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَ مِنْ رَسُولِهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ كُلِّهِمْ لِعَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) بَعْدَ وَفَاةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ كَانَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) حَكِيماً عَالِماً .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ibn Mahboub, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Yazeed Al Kunasy who said,

‘I asked Abu Ja’farasws, ‘Was Isaas Ibn Maryamas, when heas spoke in the cradle, a Divine Authority of Allahazwj upon the people of hisasws era?’ So heasws said: ‘Heas was a Prophetas in those days, a Divine Authority of Allahazwj without being a Mursil. Have you not listened to hisas words when heas said [19:30] He said: I am a servant of Allah; He has Given me the Book and Made me a Prophet [19:31] And He has Made me Blessed wherever I may be, and He has Enjoined upon me with the Prayer and the Zakat for as long as I live?’.

I said, ‘In those days he<sup>as</sup> was a Divine Authority upon Zakariyya<sup>as</sup> in that state, and he<sup>as</sup> (Isa<sup>as</sup>) was in the cradle?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Isa<sup>as</sup>, in that state, was a Sign for the people and a Mercy from Allah<sup>azwj</sup> to Maryam<sup>as</sup> when he<sup>as</sup> spoke, so he<sup>as</sup> expressed on her<sup>as</sup> behalf, and he<sup>as</sup> was a Prophet<sup>as</sup>, a Divine Authority upon the ones who heard his<sup>as</sup> speech in that state. Then he<sup>as</sup> was silent, so he<sup>as</sup> did not speak until two years passed by for him<sup>as</sup>, and it was so that Zakariyya<sup>as</sup> was the Divine Authority of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic upon the people after the silence of Isa<sup>as</sup> by two years.

Then Zakariyyaas passed away, so hisas sonas Yahyaas inherited himas, the Book and the Wisdom, and heas was a young child. Have you not listened to the Words of the Mighty and Majestic [19:12] O Yahya! Grab hold of the Book with strength; and We Granted him the Wisdom whilst he was a child?

So when Isa<sup>as</sup> reached the age of seven years, he<sup>as</sup> spoke with the Prophet-hood and the Messenger-ship when Allah<sup>azwj</sup> the Exalted Revealed unto him<sup>as</sup>. So Isa<sup>as</sup> was the Divine Authority upon Yahya<sup>as</sup> and upon the people altogether, and the earth doesn’t remain, O Abu Khalid, even for one day without a Divine Authority of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> upon the people since the day Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Created Adam<sup>as</sup> and Settled him<sup>as</sup> in the earth’.

So I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! Was Ali<sup>asws</sup> a Divine Authority of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup> upon this community during the lifetime of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, the day he<sup>saww</sup> nominated him<sup>asws</sup> to the people and established him<sup>asws</sup> as a flag and called them to his<sup>as</sup> Wilayah and ordered them with his<sup>asws</sup> obedience’.

I said, ‘And was it so that the obedience to Ali<sup>asws</sup> was an Obligation upon the people during the lifetime of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> and after his<sup>saww</sup> passing away?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, but he<sup>asws</sup> was silent, so he<sup>asws</sup> did not speak along with Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, and it was so that the obedience to Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> was upon his<sup>saww</sup> community and upon Ali<sup>asws</sup> during the lifetime of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, and it was so that the obedience for Ali<sup>asws</sup> was from Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and from His<sup>azwj</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup> upon the people, all of them, after the passing away of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, and it was so that Ali<sup>asws</sup> was wise, knowledgeable’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from ibn Mahbub from Hisham ibn Salim from Yazid al-Kunasiyy who has said the following. “I asked abu Ja‘far (a.s.), ‘Was Jesus at the time he spoke to people from the cradle a Divine authority over the people of his time?” He said, “At that time he was a prophet, a Divine authority over the people but not a Mursal (Messenger). Have you not heard the words of Allah that say, “He said, “I am the servant of God. He has given me the Book and has appointed me to be a Prophet [19:30]. He has blessed me no matter where I dwell, commanded me to worship Him and pay the religious tax for as long as I live.” [19:31] I then said, “Was he the Divine authority over Zackaria at the time that he was in the cradle?” He said, “At that time Jesus was a sign from Allah and a blessing to Mary (Maryam) when he spoke on her behalf. He was a prophet and a Divine authority over all those who heard his words at that time. Thereafter he did not speak until he was two years old. At Such time Zackaria was the Divine authority of Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most gracious, over the people after the silence of Jesus. Zackaria then died and his son Yahya (John) inherited the book and wisdom and he was a small child. You can hear it in the words of Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most gracious, “We commanded John, Zachariah’s son), “O John, follow the guidance of the Lord with due steadfastness. We gave knowledge and wisdom to John during his childhood.” [19:12] When Jesus became seven years he then spoke as a prophet and a messenger until Allah, the Most Holy sent him inspiration. Thus, Jesus was the Divine authority over John and over all the people. The earth is never left without the presence of a Divine authority over the people, O abu Khalid, from the day Allah created Adam and settled him on earth.” I then said, May Allah take my soul in service for your cause, “Did Ali (a.s.) possess Divine authority from Allah and His Messenger over this nation (Muslims) during the life time of the Messenger of Allah?” He said, “Yes, but he kept silent and did not speak along with the Messenger of Allah. At that time, during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah, obedience was of the rights of the Messenger of Allah over the ’Umma (nation) and over Ali (a.s.). Obedience on the authority of Allah and the Messenger of Allah over the people as a whole was obligatory to Ali (a.s.) after the death of the Messenger of Allah. Ali (a.s.) was a very wise and knowledgeable person.”
